context: Synthetic example. Demonstrates that the instrument can ACQUIT: a product company with operating proof and arms-length validation reads A / Clean.

claims.statement: Won a competed program of record against three named bidders.
Independently measured 32% over the stated baseline at a national test range.
Named operational customer reports repeated competitive use.
Series B of $40M disclosed in registry filing.
Production line passed an external manufacturing audit.

seams.reason: Competed awards scale with the raise.
Named, dated, competed award in hand.
Leads with measured performance against a baseline.
Competed program of record plus a returning operator.
A working system with verified results survives removal of the narrative.
Customer owns workflows and records; no private operating-layer lock.

components.reason: Competed external contract value is healthy against cumulative capital.
Validation came from an independent national test range, not a funded ranking.

verdictNotes.rationale: Competed program of record, independently verified performance, and a named returning operator. Ignore the hype where present and read the contracts: they hold.
verdictNotes.largestGap: Production scale-up beyond the audited pilot line is not yet proven.
verdictNotes.tenseOfProof: Past — awards and tests are dated and in hand.
verdictNotes.whatWouldClearIt: Already substantially cleared; a second competed award at scale would close the remaining loop.
verdictNotes.rootsBeliefSupplier: Independent test range and operational customer.
verdictNotes.rootsValueRetainer: Customer retains workflows, records, and reproducibility.
nextPulls: Production capacity audit at full rate
